# Roles Compose from Archetypes and Ship an Onboarding Roadmap

**Flexibility: Expected**

## The belief

Roles compose from a first-class, registered **archetype library** — reusable
job descriptions per role family (Auditor, Analyst, Coordinator, Specialist,
Warden…) — rather than being designed bespoke each time. And a role is not
"finished" at birth: **every birthed role ships an onboarding roadmap** —
research → test → improve → tool-up — and earns expanded authority through
review, the way a new hire ramps and is promoted.

## Why we believe this

These are the two halves the substrate was missing around birthing. On the front
end, bespoke design is wasteful and inconsistent; archetypes (JOS-P12 Design
Once, Compose Many, applied to roles) make the next hire cheap and uniform, and
they are *created* by compressing observed role behavior (JOS-C08 / JOS-C33). On
the back end, treating birth as the finish line is the classic failure — real
competence comes from the ramp: probing the role, fixing what breaks, acquiring
the tools it turns out to need, and climbing the trust ladder (JOS-P01) on
evidence (JOS-P08 Audit-and-Learn). Birthing and the trust-climb are one
lifecycle, not two disconnected events.

## What this looks like in practice

- An archetype/role-template library exists and is the default starting point;
  new roles instantiate an archetype and specialize it.
- A role's onboarding roadmap is a real artifact: test plan, known gaps,
  tool-acquisition list, and trust-ladder rungs with the evidence to advance.
- Post-birth retrospectives feed both the role (improve it) and the library
  (compress its proven patterns into the archetype for the next hire).

## What this rules out

- Hand-building every agent or hire from zero with no reusable role family.
- Declaring a role "done" at birth with no plan to test, improve, or tool it up.
- A static trust level that never moves on evidence.

## Connected Objects

- **[JOS-C53]** Agents in an Org Chart (founds this Principle).
- **[JOS-P12]** Design Once, Compose Many (archetype reuse).
- **[JOS-C08]** Compression Gradient (observed roles → archetypes).
- **[JOS-P01]** Trust & Graduated Autonomy (the trust-ladder climb).
- **[JOS-P08]** Audit and Learn (the improve loop).
